text:
Once upon a very long ago, in a land filled with talking animals, there lived a wise old owl named Otus. Each evening after the sun had settled down to rest, he would embark on his favorite pastime: listening to the gossip of the critter community.

One evening, after a particularly lengthy exchange about mice, Otus remarked, "You know, they say gossiping endlessly makes for fascinating company."

A little pigeon replied skeptically, "But why gossip about mice? They wouldn't miss us."

"I've heard," Otus said gravely, "that mice adore quiet evenings. Just the same thing as us. Especially their stories about other cities."

Curious, the little bird fluttered closer. "Oh! You read stories?" she asked eagerly.

"All sorts. Tales of mice kingdoms far away, wise owl elder pals, adventures…"

Suddenly, there was a soft flicker nearby. A mouse hopped excitedly. "Oh my! You heard those stories?"
